# ARM SVE / SVE2 Quick Reference

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_arm_intrinsics_manual.py. Do not edit manually. -->

面向长度无关循环的 SVE / SVE2 quick reference，聚焦 svcnt*、谓词、predicated load/store、广播、加法、FMA、horizontal reduction 和 dot-product。

## Headers and Feature Gates

| Header | Feature Gate | Typical Use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| <arm_sve.h> | `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE` | 逐元素加法 |
| <arm_sve.h> | `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE2` | SVE2 点积 |

## Predicate and Vector-Length Primitives

| Intrinsic | Maps To | Use For | Key Constraint |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `svcntw` | `CNTW` | 获取当前 VL | Use svcntw() or the matching svcnt*() form to keep the loop vector-length agnostic. |
| `svwhilelt_b32` | `WHILELT` | 生成当前步谓词 | Build a fresh predicate from the current scalar index and loop bound each iteration. |

## Load / Store Patterns

| Intrinsic | Maps To | Use For | Key Constraint |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `svld1_f32` | `LD1`, `LD1W` | 连续读取向量主体 | Pass a valid svbool_t predicate as the first argument. |
| `svst1_f32` | `ST1`, `ST1W` | 写回向量主体 | Keep the store predicate aligned with the compute predicate. |

## Arithmetic and Broadcast

| Intrinsic | Maps To | Use For | Key Constraint |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `svdup_n_f32` | `DUP` | 广播标量到向量 | Use the matching scalar type for the target vector element width. |
| `svadd_f32_x` | `ADD`, `ADD (vectors, predicated)` | 逐元素加法 | Match the element type of both operands and the destination vector. |
| `svmla_f32_x` | `FMLA`, `MLA` | 乘加或累加内核 | Use svmla_f32_x when the scalar logic is an accumulate += lhs * rhs pattern. |

## Reduction Helpers

| Intrinsic | Maps To | Use For | Key Constraint |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `svaddv_f32` | `FADDV` | sum/dot 最终水平归约 | Use for final reduction of an SVE vector accumulator, not for scan output. |
| `svaddv_s32` | `SADDV` | sum/dot 最终水平归约 | Use only when int32 overflow behavior is either irrelevant or explicitly acceptable. |

## SVE2 Extensions

| Intrinsic | Maps To | Use For | Key Constraint |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `svdot_s32` | `SDOT`, `SDOT (vectors)` | SVE2 点积 | Gate usage on __ARM_FEATURE_SVE2. |

## Loop Control Pattern

```c
#include <arm_sve.h>
#include <stdint.h>

void add_arrays_f32_sve(const float *a, const float *b, float *out, int n) {
    const int vl = (int)svcntw();
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i += vl) {
        svbool_t pg = svwhilelt_b32((uint64_t)i, (uint64_t)n);
        svfloat32_t va = svld1_f32(pg, a + i);
        svfloat32_t vb = svld1_f32(pg, b + i);
        svfloat32_t vc = svadd_f32_x(pg, va, vb);
        svst1_f32(pg, out + i, vc);
    }
}
```

## Common Pitfalls

- `Do not mix NEON, SVE and SME templates in one kernel body`: Calling vld1q_f32 and svld1_f32 in the same loop body.
- `SVE and SME intrinsics must include the matching header`: Using svld1_f32 or svzero_za() with no Arm SVE or SME header include.
- `SVE loads and stores must show predicate coverage`: Using svld1_* or svst1_* with no visible predicate generation.
- `SVE loops must be vector-length agnostic`: Using i += 4 or another fixed lane count in an SVE loop.

## Quick Lookup

### `svcntw`

- `Maps To`: `CNTW`
- `Prototype`: `uint64_t svcntw(void)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 获取当前 VL
- `Key Rule`: Use svcntw() or the matching svcnt*() form to keep the loop vector-length agnostic.
- `Common Mistake`: Hard-coding i += 4 in an SVE loop.
- `Example`:
```c
const int vl = (int)svcntw();
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / List of SVE intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svwhilelt_b32`

- `Maps To`: `WHILELT`
- `Prototype`: `svbool_t svwhilelt_b32(uint64_t op1, uint64_t op2)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 生成当前步谓词
- `Key Rule`: Build a fresh predicate from the current scalar index and loop bound each iteration.
- `Common Mistake`: Generating pg once outside the loop and reusing it for all iterations.
- `Example`:
```c
svbool_t pg = svwhilelt_b32((uint64_t)i, (uint64_t)n);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svld1_f32`

- `Maps To`: `LD1`, `LD1W`
- `Prototype`: `svfloat32_t svld1_f32(svbool_t pg, float32_t const *base)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 连续读取向量主体
- `Key Rule`: Pass a valid svbool_t predicate as the first argument.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling svld1_f32 without a predicate value.
- `Example`:
```c
svfloat32_t va = svld1_f32(pg, a + i);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svst1_f32`

- `Maps To`: `ST1`, `ST1W`
- `Prototype`: `void svst1_f32(svbool_t pg, float32_t *base, svfloat32_t data)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 写回向量主体
- `Key Rule`: Keep the store predicate aligned with the compute predicate.
- `Common Mistake`: Using an unpredicated store in a scalable-width loop.
- `Example`:
```c
svst1_f32(pg, out + i, vc);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svdup_n_f32`

- `Maps To`: `DUP`
- `Prototype`: `svfloat32_t svdup_n_f32(float32_t value)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 广播标量到向量
- `Key Rule`: Use the matching scalar type for the target vector element width.
- `Common Mistake`: Treating svdup_n_f32 like a fixed 4-lane NEON duplicate.
- `Example`:
```c
svfloat32_t vlhs = svdup_n_f32(lhs[row * k + depth]);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / List of SVE intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svadd_f32_x`

- `Maps To`: `ADD`, `ADD (vectors, predicated)`
- `Prototype`: `svfloat32_t svadd_f32_x(svbool_t pg, svfloat32_t op1, svfloat32_t op2)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 逐元素加法
- `Key Rule`: Match the element type of both operands and the destination vector.
- `Common Mistake`: Dropping the predicate argument or reusing a stale predicate.
- `Example`:
```c
svfloat32_t vc = svadd_f32_x(pg, va, vb);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svmla_f32_x`

- `Maps To`: `FMLA`, `MLA`
- `Prototype`: `svfloat32_t svmla_f32_x(svbool_t pg, svfloat32_t acc, svfloat32_t lhs, svfloat32_t rhs)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: 乘加或累加内核
- `Key Rule`: Use svmla_f32_x when the scalar logic is an accumulate += lhs * rhs pattern.
- `Common Mistake`: Using svmla_f32_x for a plain add without multiplication.
- `Example`:
```c
vacc = svmla_f32_x(pg, vacc, vlhs, vrhs);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svaddv_f32`

- `Maps To`: `FADDV`
- `Prototype`: `float32_t svaddv_f32(svbool_t pg, svfloat32_t op)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: sum/dot 最终水平归约
- `Key Rule`: Use for final reduction of an SVE vector accumulator, not for scan output.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling svaddv_f32 while the scalar contract requires strict left-to-right floating-point order.
- `Example`:
```c
float acc = svaddv_f32(svptrue_b32(), vacc);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svaddv_s32`

- `Maps To`: `SADDV`
- `Prototype`: `int32_t svaddv_s32(svbool_t pg, svint32_t op)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE`
- `Use When`: sum/dot 最终水平归约
- `Key Rule`: Use only when int32 overflow behavior is either irrelevant or explicitly acceptable.
- `Common Mistake`: Vectorizing an int32 sum when the original semantics require trapping or checked overflow.
- `Example`:
```c
int32_t acc = svaddv_s32(svptrue_b32(), vacc);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12

### `svdot_s32`

- `Maps To`: `SDOT`, `SDOT (vectors)`
- `Prototype`: `svint32_t svdot_s32(svint32_t acc, svint8_t lhs, svint8_t rhs)`
- `Header`: `<arm_sve.h>`
- `Feature Gate`: `__ARM_FEATURE_SVE2`
- `Use When`: SVE2 点积
- `Key Rule`: Gate usage on __ARM_FEATURE_SVE2.
- `Common Mistake`: Calling svdot_s32 in plain SVE-only code without an SVE2 feature check.
- `Example`:
```c
acc = svdot_s32(acc, lhs_i8, rhs_i8);
```

- `Source`: [Arm C Language Extensions](https://arm-software.github.io/acle/main/acle.html) / SVE language extensions and intrinsics / Mapping of SVE instructions to intrinsics / 2026-05-12
